Hey, welcome back. All right, so what do we do now? We launched those level one campaigns I also like to call those our seed campaigns So let's talk about scaling those suckers and making them work better now chances are Not every campaign worked out perfectly and that's totally normal and that's what we're gonna start with right now in this video So our mission for this section is to take our campaigns from level one Which are those seed campaigns to level two? This is when we start scaling campaigns and this is when we start trimming away bad stuff Also known as like what the heck do we do now? So I understand that maybe Probably almost definitely not all of your ad sets actually worked and I know for sure That not all campaigns are going to work in the long run things are gonna break So I want to go ahead and address some of those issues now So in case you're worried or weirded out you won't be okay So like I'm prematurely potentially giving you this how to troubleshoot campaigns information But I want you to know that if it didn't immediately make profit for you the stuff We built in our last section together. It really wasn't supposed to and everything's gonna be fine. So thing number one All campaigns will eventually go south. All right, they all will eventually stop working if this happens Or I should actually say when this happens Do not panic. All right, don't let it freak you out. There are ways to fix it All right, so first this address why they might be going bad So typically what will happen especially with Facebook is really true with almost every ad network in the world It's actually I can't think of an ad work or an ad network where this isn't true What eventually will happen is if you don't change the ad Over time they'll just start charging you more because they want a good user experience and they're like wait a minute If the person keeps seeing the same ad all the time over and over and over again, they're gonna get bored So we can't make this person change their ad but we can start charging them more and then they'll change their ad because it'll quit Working so to speak so a lot of times a campaign or an ad set or an audience or whatever It'll just stop working and all you got to do is change your ad up a little bit and then that'll fix it But sometimes it's other reasons right and so here's what to do First you want to choke the budgets on bad ad sets now I know I'm giving you this video right after we just made our seed campaigns But during our time together this week, I'm actually going to show you some campaigns I've just been let running for like several weeks now. I've just totally let them run Unattended so some of them could go bad so I could show you what to do So this is kind of like an overview of what to do, you know So test number one is you can always just choke the budgets back down So if an audience was working and then it stops working you can just lower the bid from wherever it was Let's say you'd scaled it up to 100 bucks a day or something you can lower that back down to $5 a day and then Dine thing might start working again a lot of times that happens and you can start scaling it back up The second test that you can do is like I mentioned earlier just refresh the creative That's a fancy way of saying create a new ad No, a lot of times I'll run the exact same ad and I'll just add an extra sentence of copy or I'll change the picture a little bit Like I'll change a color filter on the picture or something like that a third thing you can test is you can turn the entire Campaign off for a day and then turn it back on this is literally like Unplugging your computer and then plugging it back in if it's not running right. It's the same thing Sometimes that resets the algorithm and your fourth test it can just rebuild the entire campaign from scratch Exactly like it originally was and just start out with the original low budgets and then just build it back up Now all four of those things Generally speaking one of them will work when a campaign's been working or an ad sets been working or whatever and it stops working One of these things typically work. We just don't know which one it will be. That's why I call them tests Okay, so, you know, they're not guaranteed to work but generally speaking one of them are going to work no matter what and No matter what here's some things we should always be doing now Keep in mind our time together during this session is we're gonna scale these campaigns I just built and I'm gonna show you how to fix campaigns I've been running for a while that I've kind of deliberately let go bad But I'm just kind of giving you a big picture of next level stuff now So some things you always want to be doing number one You always want to be running new campaigns to new audiences using the method that I taught you last week Right and so I call that the miser method where we take, you know, one simple ad or maybe a fancy ad Whatever then we run it to a whole bunch of good audiences and we see what happens because the more good audiences you have the more money You will ultimately make alright, so you always want to be doing that You always want to allocate a little bit of your budget to that The second thing you always want to be doing is you always want to be looking for trends. Okay, and this is like Absolutely positively T totally critical Okay, so here's the thing You just created a campaign and if you're if you're new at this then it's real easy to fall into this trap Where you might have a campaign that doesn't work right for a day or maybe even two days and it freaks you out And you're like, oh my gosh, it doesn't work. Everything's horrible. All right, so look for trends three state straight days of bad performance Is a trend. Okay one day of bad performance is nothing It's an off day and the more traffic you get and the more sales or transactions or leads or whatever that are coming through your sales process the more accurate Your data is going to be meaning if I make a decision based off, you know 100 people buy and my upsell take rate is low versus a thousand people buy and my upsell take rate is low Well that decision based off of a thousand buyers is going to be more accurate because this has more data behind it All right, and you always just want to use math. Okay, so if I know if a customer is typically worth like let's say 25 bucks No, in my case, it's worth 34 98. I'm gonna let it keep running or I'm gonna panic and change something really really quickly when in fact like usually We just let it ride. We're gonna be okay.
